description: A small but highly engaged group with strong purchase intent, though their activity is sporadic and spread across multiple categories. They view and add many items to cart, convert at high rates, and almost always make purchases, but do so infrequently and with long gaps between sessions and orders. Their purchase behavior is more focused than their browsing, and they show moderate alignment between viewed and purchased categories.
evidence: Average of 14.63 page views per session
Average of 5.22 add-to-cart actions per session
99.7% purchaser ratio (nearly all make at least one purchase)
Top view categories: Books (23.6%), Beauty (18.6%), Toys (16.9%)
Top purchase categories: Books (27.3%), Beauty (23.1%), Toys (15.2%)
cautions: This segment was identified via KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating it is a behavior-based approximation rather than a distinct natural group
The name 'Hesitant' reflects long inactivity periods (738.6 days since last session, 1020.1 days since last order) but does not imply psychological trait

description: A segment of customers who exhibit high engagement with the platform, frequent purchases across diverse categories, and consistent view-to-purchase alignment, though with longer gaps between orders and last session.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.2%; Toys: 15.3%
Top purchase categories: Books: 30.2%; Beauty: 25.2%; Toys: 15.3%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with generally low silhouette scores, so it should be treated as a behavior-based approximation rather than a sharply separated natural group.
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels due to the clustering method's limitations.

description: A segment of customers who frequently view and add items to cart but do not make purchases, with a high average add-to-cart rate and a long inactivity period. They show interest in specific product categories but do not complete transactions.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 20.2%; Beauty: 19.0%; Toys: 13.8%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating it is a behavior-based approximation, not a sharply separated group.

description: Customers who frequently browse multiple categories, add items to cart at a moderate rate, purchase all items added to cart, but never buy from the same category they viewed most. They have high category diversity in purchases and views, with no overlap between top view and purchase categories.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 18.5%; Toys: 17.2%; Beauty: 15.8%
Top purchase categories: Beauty: 24.4%; Books: 23.5%; Toys: 14.2%
cautions: Segment derived from KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores—interpret as behavior approximation, not distinct natural group
Label avoids demographic/lifestyle inferences (income, age, occupation, etc.) due to lack of observable data

description: A large segment of customers who frequently browse and add items to cart but make very few purchases, with a long average time since their last order. They have a high add-to-cart rate and high purchase category concentration, but no view-purchase category match. They are 100% purchasers but with very low order frequency and total spend.
evidence: Top view categories: Books: 19.3%; Beauty: 15.8%; Toys: 15.0%
Top purchase categories: Books: 21.7%; Beauty: 20.1%; Toys: 15.8%
cautions: This segment was produced by KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, so it should be treated as a behavior-based approximation, not a sharply separated natural group
Avoid overconfident or marketing-style labels as the segment boundaries are not precise

description: A segment of customers who consistently view and purchase from a small set of dominant categories, with high add-to-cart rates and total purchase conversion, but low order frequency and long inactivity periods.
evidence: 100.0% purchaser ratio (all customers in this segment made at least one purchase)
1.53 average purchase category diversity (very low diversity)
35.7% average dominant view category ratio (moderate focus)
83.8% average dominant purchase category ratio (extreme focus)
Top view categories: Books (26.2%), Beauty (21.6%), Toys (15.6%)
Top purchase categories: Books (26.2%), Beauty (21.6%), Toys (15.6%)
cautions: This segment was identified through KMeans clustering with low silhouette scores, indicating it is a behavior-based approximation rather than a sharply defined group
The long inactivity periods (327.8 days since last session, 986.5 days since last order) suggest this may not be a currently active segment
